summary: Twelfth pass eliminating implicit-function-declaration warnings, with the PR body stating that `gr` and `it` were done. The change mainly tightened prototypes and includes across ground/stage and item code, converted many stage callback stubs from K&R/empty parameter lists or `s32` return placeholders to explicit typed signatures, added missing public declarations, and adjusted compile/editor warning handling. Review discussion focused on preserving readability and matching intent while satisfying clang/GCC constraints.
what_changed: Added or corrected function declarations so `-Werror=implicit-function-declaration` could be enforced across the touched `gr` and `it` areas.
Added `src/melee/gr/forward.h` with a `MELEE_GR_FORWARD_H` guard and a dependency on `Runtime/platform.h`.
Added missing declarations such as `bool func_801C83D0(HSD_GObj*, bool, enum_t);` in `granime.h` and `bool func_8021A10C();` in `grbattle.h`.

Updated many ground/stage callback prototypes from implicit or loose forms like `static void func()` and `static s32 callback(void)` to explicit signatures such as `static void func(HSD_GObj*)`, `static bool callback(HSD_GObj*)`, and `static HSD_GObj* func(int gobj_id)`.
Changed boolean-returning stage callbacks from numeric `s32`/`0`/`1` style to `bool` with `false`/`true` returns where the diff showed callback semantics.
Replaced several raw flag constants with clearer bit expressions or shared macros, including adding `FLAGS_NONE`, `HAS_FLAG(value, flag)`, and `NOT_FLAG(value, flag)` in `platform.h`.
Added `-Wno-gnu-folding-constant` to `compile_flags.txt`, while review discussion also noted a related clang issue when using a `static const int` as an array bound.
Renamed at least one anonymous argument to a semantic name in fighter code: `func_80086368(..., f32 arg8)` became `func_80086368(..., f32 facing_dir)`.
For matching-sensitive unused stack slots, replaced plain unused arrays with `/// @todo Unused stack.` comments guarded by `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` and byte-sized arrays in several ground functions.
Added small Doxygen stub files such as `grbattle.dox`, `item.dox`, and `types.dox` for private or documented functions/types.
smart_moves: Treated implicit declaration cleanup as a header/prototype ownership problem rather than just suppressing warnings, adding includes and declarations at the apparent subsystem boundaries.
Made callback signatures match their actual call contracts by adding `HSD_GObj*` parameters to many no-op callbacks, which prevents the compiler from assuming old-style unspecified parameters.
Used `bool`, `true`, and `false` for stage callback results that behaved as predicates, improving type information while reducing warning pressure.
Introduced shared flag macros in `platform.h` so repeated flag checks can be expressed consistently instead of scattering ad hoc comparisons to zero.
Preserved matching-only stack artifacts behind `MUST_MATCH` instead of deleting them outright, keeping source readability while retaining a path for exact matching.
Responded to review feedback on `HAS_FLAG`/`NOT_FLAG` by adopting the simpler C-style truthiness form suggested by the reviewer.
Resolved the `BATTLE_BG_MAX` array-bound discussion by recognizing that a `const` object is not equivalent to a preprocessor constant for all compiler warning modes.
decomp_lessons: When enabling `-Werror=implicit-function-declaration`, expect fixes to cascade through headers: missing prototypes often reveal incorrect callback types, wrong return types, and missing includes.
Old-style `void func()` declarations are risky in C decomp code; use `void func(void)` or the actual argument list to satisfy strict prototype and implicit declaration checks.
Stage callback tables are a useful source of truth for callback signatures: if table entries are invoked with `HSD_GObj*`, even empty callbacks should accept `HSD_GObj*`.
Do not model predicate callbacks as `s32` unless evidence requires it; `bool` plus `true`/`false` can better communicate behavior and avoid mismatched declarations.
For compiler-portable constants used in array bounds, a `#define` or enum constant may be safer than `static const int` under clang warnings such as `-Wgnu-folding-constant`.
Matching scaffolding should be explicit and fenced: annotate unused stack with `@todo` and guard with `MUST_MATCH` instead of leaving unexplained dummy locals.
Reviewer readability concerns matter even during warning cleanup; avoid odd-looking rewrites such as replacing an empty `do { } while` with a bracketless `do continue; while` form unless necessary.
naming_conventions: Use semantic parameter names where evidence is clear, e.g. `facing_dir` instead of `arg8` in `func_80086368` and `gobj_id` instead of a generic integer for stage object IDs.
Header guards follow subsystem/file naming, shown by `MELEE_GR_FORWARD_H` for `src/melee/gr/forward.h`.
Flag helper macros are uppercase: `FLAGS_NONE`, `HAS_FLAG(value, flag)`, and `NOT_FLAG(value, flag)`.
Ground callback placeholder names remained address-derived or label-derived (`func_8021A10C`, `lbl_8020E078`), but their prototypes were made more descriptive through parameter and return types.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Kept unused stack allocation only under `#ifdef MUST_MATCH`, using byte arrays sized to the original stack reservation such as `u8 unused[8]`, `u8 unused[24]`, or `u8 unused[28]`.
Added explicit casts/types only where still unresolved, with comments such as `/// @todo Missing cast` and temporary `unk_t hsd_obj = gobj->hsd_obj` in `grbattle.c`.
Changed raw numeric boolean returns to `false`/`true`, which can preserve codegen intent while making type contracts match headers.
Used explicit bit shifts like `(1 << 30) | (1 << 31)` and `FLAGS_NONE` instead of some raw hex/`0UL` initializers in stage data/callback tables.
Added `-Wno-gnu-folding-constant` in `compile_flags.txt`, but review indicates future code should prefer true compile-time constants when array bounds need to remain explicit.

review_feedback.feedback: Keep brackets on an empty `do { } while` loop; the proposed `do continue; while` form looked too weird.
Author acknowledged the lbfile change was accidental and agreed to keep both readability and intended form.
Suggested simpler macro definitions: `HAS_FLAG(value, flag) ((value) & (flag))` and `NOT_FLAG(value, flag) (!((value) & (flag)))`.
Author accepted the C-style truthiness preference despite noting it differs from explicit comparisons used in some languages.
Preferred keeping `s16 sp28[BATTLE_BG_MAX] = { 1, 2, 4 };` because the named bound makes iteration clearer.
Explained clang warned because `BATTLE_BG_MAX` was `const` rather than a define; planned to change that so both compilers would accept the clearer bounded array.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove `-Wno-gnu-folding-constant` or convert `static const int` bounds to `#define`/enum constants.
Search for remaining old-style declarations `func()` in `gr` and `it` after PR 681.
Search for remaining `s32` predicate callbacks in `StageCallbacks` tables that should be `bool`.
Check whether `src/melee/gr/forward.h` later gained actual forward declarations beyond including `Runtime/platform.h`.
Audit `MUST_MATCH` unused stack annotations introduced in this pass for eventual removal or better type reconstruction.
